next_step_text: June 15 Rick: Conducted Intake Demo to Alex (info gatherer, reports to Nathan w/findings) Short-term focus: enable intake for easier end‑user adoption Longer-term goal: expand into sourcing/contract automation as organizational readiness improves Follow up: Horizon invite, Showpad w/demo-recording.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Alex Becker to report Merlin Intake evaluation findings to Nathan Hemmer (Sr. Corp. Director of Indirect Procurement Global) and secure Nathan's strategic decision on priority and adoption timeline.
Fortive to evaluate Horizon event attendance (Sept 21–23, Beaver Creek) for indirect sourcing/commodity team leads and confirm whether they will attend.

competitive_position.summary: Jagger (incumbent, 8 years) is OUT — already displaced by signed Zycus S2P phase-one contract, lost on product stagnation and vendor disengagement. Do-nothing/delay is the LIVE competitive threat (highest current risk): buyer's stated adoption timeline is 'late 27–28 initiative' with 1–2 year org-wide rollout due to legacy-user resistance, InfoSec gatekeeping, and multi-ERP complexity. Zycus is sole-source for Merlin Intake (no peer vendor evaluation), but organizational readiness constraints and 'last-mile AI adoption' challenges (Alex, 12 Jun) create deprioritization risk. Wedge: prove adoption lift in a Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 pilot with indirect sourcing power users, leverage Scott Anderson's ISM-demo enthusiasm and Horizon peer validation (Sept 21–23) to create executive pull, and position Merlin Intake as the 'interface-agnostic, no-training' UX solution that solves Jagger's failure and de-risks the broader 2027–2028 rollout.

competitors.name: Jagger (incumbent)
competitors.quote: we had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years... the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated.
competitors.how_we_win: Already being displaced in phase one; reinforce Zycus innovation pace (single source code, 50% of staff in development) vs Jagger stagnation.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / Delay
competitors.quote: I'm gonna say that is, like, a late 27 28 initiative... we're only a year or two out on that.
competitors.how_we_win: Carve out a small pilot with indirect sourcing power users to prove adoption lift now and keep momentum via Horizon (Sept 21-23) and quarterly roadmap cadence.

competitors.name: Jagger
competitors.quote: we had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years… there is some change cost… contracts are signed … guys come from … the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated
competitors.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Alex Becker + Rick Taranek
competitors.how_we_win: Jagger already lost the platform decision (Phase 1 Zycus eProcurement signed and in implementation). Not a threat for Merlin Intake.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / Delay (organizational readiness)
competitors.quote: I'm gonna say that is, like, a late 27–28 initiative… we have to see when the crowd would be ready for a change like that… the last mile AI problem that we're all going through. We spent billions of dollars for AI infrastructure, and we are still having issues seeing the impact on the P&L because individual productivity is increasing, but how does that translate into dollars… just getting people to use a chat interface… all bigger organizations are struggling with that because, a, it's like user adoption and also, b, IT being okay with what is happening here in regards to information security. So it'll be a little while before we get users to create a requisition in the chat, but I think we're only a year or two out.
competitors.how_we_win: Prove adoption lift in a small, controlled pilot (indirect sourcing power users, Q4 2026 or Q1 2027) that quantifies ROI (user-adoption %, cycle-time reduction, rogue-spend capture) and de-risks InfoSec concerns (leverage Microsoft Teams certified-app status, Zycus's existing Fortive deployment trust). Bring Alex + Nathan + indirect sourcing leads to Horizon (Sept 21–23) for peer validation and executive sponsorship. Position Merlin Intake as 'interface-agnostic, no-training, easier for the user' (solving Jagger's UX failure) and frame it as Phase 1a of the already-signed S2P conversion (momentum, not new initiative). The activation: co-author the pilot plan and internal business case with Alex in the next 14 days, secure Nathan's strategic approval by mid-Aug, and target pilot kickoff Q4 2026.

competitors.name: Jagger (Jaggaer) — incumbent being displaced
competitors.quote: we had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years... the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated... the industry is changing, they don't care
competitors.how_we_win: Reinforce Zycus's single-source-code, 50%-in-R&D innovation cadence and quarterly releases against Jagger's perceived stagnation.

competitors.name: Jagger (Jaggaer)
competitors.quote: We had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years since I joined the company. Jagger doesn't like to develop… the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated. The industry is changing. They don't care. I totally get why you made the jump.
competitors.source: Rick Taranek + Alex Becker confirmed, call 2026-06-12
competitors.how_we_win: Already won the base S2P platform displacement (phase-one contract signed). Jagger is OUT. Legacy-user familiarity with Jagger forms ('I can fill this form out within 15 seconds') feeds do-nothing resistance, but Jagger is not in the running for Merlin Intake.

champion_strength.summary: Champion developing; Alex Becker is enthusiastic, actively advocating, and requesting ongoing engagement (Horizon, roadmap updates), but his influence with Nathan Hemmer (strategic decision-maker) and ability to drive commercial approval are unproven. Access to Economic Buyer unknown.
champion_strength.champion: Alex Becker (Director of Business Intelligence, Strategic Sourcing)

sources: Nathan Hemmer, call 2026-05-07 (strategic scope, not budget confirmed)
Scott Anderson referenced as VP (tasked evaluation, not yet engaged on EB capacity)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer unmapped; no budget authority identified, no commercial or pricing conversation has occurred. Alex Becker is Director of Business Intelligence (procurement business partner, analytics/BI/systems scope) and explicitly stated 'I am the procurement business partner… I would never say I'm a sourcing guy because I don't negotiate with suppliers' (12 Jun call) — he is an info gatherer and influencer, not the budget-holder. Nathan Hemmer (Sr. Corp. Director of Indirect Procurement Global) is the strategic decision-maker who will 'set the strategic sail' (Alex, 12 Jun call), but Nathan's scope is strategy and prioritization, and no evidence he controls the commercial/budget approval for a $60k SaaS add-on. The EB is genuinely unknown. Fortive is a large, multi-ERP, 20–30 business-unit organization; the budget authority likely sits at VP Procurement (Scott Anderson, who tasked the evaluation) or CFO/finance level. Amount $60,000 is logged in Salesforce but has never been discussed with any buyer contact. The move: ask Alex and Nathan directly who approves budget for new procurement technology/SaaS and map that person into the evaluation.

recommended_moves: Co-author a one-page pilot business case with Alex Becker (adoption-lift target, Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 pilot scope with 10–20 indirect sourcing users, 90-day proof window, quantified ROI metrics: user-adoption %, requisition cycle-time reduction, rogue-spend capture) that Alex can present to Nathan Hemmer for strategic approval.

Request a three-way checkpoint call (Rick + Alex + Nathan Hemmer) by mid-Aug to confirm Nathan's strategic priority, align on Q4 2026 pilot target, and map Economic Buyer and approval chain for commercial discussion.

Deliver Horizon event invitation package to Alex Becker (dates Sept 21–23, Beaver Creek Colorado location, agenda, peer-validation sessions, registration link) and invite Alex + Nathan + indirect sourcing leads to attend for roadmap visibility and executive pull.
